channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Directioners mourning singer Liam Payne leave tributes in London and other cities
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Hundreds gathered Sunday in London’s Kensington Gardens to mourn Liam Payne as fans around the world grieved for the One Direction singer who died this past week.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump works the fry station and holds a drive-thru news conference at a Pennsylvania McDonald's
item.description: FEASTERVILLE-TREVOSE, Pa. (AP) — Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump manned the fry station at a McDonald's in Pennsylvania on Sunday before staging an impromptu news conference, answering questions through the drive-thru window.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Thelma Mothershed Wait, a member of the Little Rock Nine who integrated an Arkansas school, has died
item.description: Thelma Mothershed Wair, one of the nine Black students who integrated a high school in Arkansas' capital city of Little Rock in 1957 while a mob of white segregationists yelled threats and insults, has died at age 83.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Moldovan president appears set to win 1st round of presidential race, but EU vote could be rejected
item.description: CHISINAU, Moldova (AP) — Moldova’s pro-Western president early Monday looked set to win the first round of a presidential race but accused “criminal groups” of undermining a referendum asking voters to decide whether to secure a path toward European Union membership, which risked being narrowly reje
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 2 Navy aviators are declared dead after a fighter jet crashed in Washington state
item.description: Two crew members who were missing following the crash of a fighter jet in mountainous terrain in Washington state during a routine training flight have been declared dead, the U.S. Navy said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump overtakes Harris for first time in Decision Desk HQ/The Hill election forecast
item.description: The model predicts Trump has a 52 percent chance of winning the presidency while Harris has a 42 percent chance, as of Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Oscar makes landfall in eastern Cuba after striking the Bahamas
item.description: Hurricane Oscar has made landfall in eastern Cuba, an island beleaguered by a massive power outage, after striking the southeastern Bahamas. The National Hurricane Center in Miami says the storm's center arrived in the Cuban province of Guantanamo, near the city of Baracoa, on Sunday evening.
item.dc:creator: AP, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Social Security benefits: What young people should know
item.description: The Social Security Administration continually tries to remind people of all ages that it pays benefit to more than just retirees.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Texas' junk science law is getting another look over Robert Roberson's case
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — When Robert Roberson's execution was abruptly halted in Texas, it was due to a subpoena ordering him to testify over a legal backstop that both Republicans and Democrats say should had saved him long ago: Texas’ junk science law.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Rock & Roll Hall of Fame turns up starpower to induct Cher, Foreigner, Mary J. Blige, Ozzy and more
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Hollywood stars Julia Roberts and Zendaya bookended Saturday’s inductions into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, giving a little stardust to an eclectic lineup that included pop icon Cher, heavy metal’s Ozzy Osbourne, hip-hop soul queen Mary J.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Popular Florida restaurant burns down on day it reopened after hurricane
item.description: A popular restaurant in Cape Coral burned down on the same day it had reopened after Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, ABC 4

item.title: Mormon church now offering sleeveless garments for members in warmer climates
item.description: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints is now offering sleeveless garments for members in hot and humid climates, according to a Church spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Mahon, KREX 5

item.title: 2 Navy aviators are declared dead after a fighter jet crashed in Washington state
item.description: Two crew members who were missing following the crash of a fighter jet in mountainous terrain in Washington state during a routine training flight have been declared dead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top 5 ways education would look different under Harris vs. Trump
item.description: Education issues have flown under the radar this election cycle despite both major candidates having big ideas about how the K-12 and college systems should work.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: How the 2025 Social Security COLA compares to recent years
item.description: A recent survey from the Motley Fool shows 50% of retirees have considered going back to work to make ends meet.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: FBI searching for missing Montana teen who vanished from home weeks ago
item.description: Sa’Wade Birdinground, 13, went missing from her home in Garryowen, Montana, on Oct. 6 and has not been heard from since, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Many gift-buying parents pressured to overspend this holiday season: Survey
item.description: (NerdWallet) - Holiday shopping season is upon us, and the pressure is on, or at least it feels that way for some parents.
item.dc:creator: Erin El Issa, KTLA 5

item.title: Romance scams on the rise as Americans look to dating apps for love: 5 tips to protect yourself
item.description: Almost 70,000 people fell victim to romance scams in 2022, according to the Federal Trade Commission. Experts told Fox News Digital how those dating online can protect themselves.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A drone targets the Israeli prime minister's house during new barrages with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's government said a drone targeted Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s house Saturday, with no casualties, as fighting with Lebanon-based Hezbollah and Gaza -based Hamas showed no pause after the killing of the Hamas mastermind of last year’s Oct. 7 attack.
item.dc:creator: ADAM SCHRECK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At least 7 dead after ferry dock gangway collapses on Georgia's Sapelo Island
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Authorities said at least seven people were killed Saturday when part of a ferry dock collapsed on Georgia's Sapelo Island, where crowds had gathered for a fall celebration by the island's tiny Gullah-Geechee community of Black slave descendants.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 people killed on same Austin, Texas street within one week
item.description: Three people were killed on Burton Drive in southeast Austin within one week, according to the Austin Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Brianna Hollis, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats look to quash threat from Green Party's Jill Stein
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over Green Party candidate Jill Stein as they look to avoid a repeat of the 2016 presidential election, in which Stein was accused of playing spoiler in key swing states. For months, Democrats had trained their ire on Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Oscar forms in the Caribbean
item.description: The formation of Oscar and Nadine comes as parts of the U.S. have been hit hard by a pair of hurricanes, Helene and Milton, that devastated communities, destroyed roads, caused power outages, took lives and injured many.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Massachusetts parents sue school district over student receiving 'D' after using AI for social studies project
item.description: A Massachusetts couple has filed a lawsuit against their son's school district after he used artificial intelligence for a class project, and was accused of plagiarism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maher: October surprise wont 'save us' from second Trump term
item.description: "Democrats need to stop thinking an October surprise is going to save us from a second Trump term," he said Friday on "Real Time with Bill Maher," referencing an event that could take place to influence the election.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: NYC officials envision turning Fifth Avenue into a grand boulevard
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s famed luxury store row Fifth Avenue is in line for a major makeover. New York City officials unveiled a plan this week to transform a central portion of the thoroughfare between Bryant Park and Central Park into a more pedestrian-centered boulevard.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stanford psychologist behind the controversial "Stanford Prison Experiment" dies at 91
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Philip G. Zimbardo, the psychologist behind the controversial “Stanford Prison Experiment” that was intended to examine the psychological experiences of imprisonment, has died. He was 91. Stanford University announced Friday that Zimbardo died Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minimum wage on the ballot in these 5 states
item.description: The federal minimum wage hasn't changed since 2009 but voters in several states will have the chance to raise hourly wages at the state level.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: Mexican cartel leader 'El Mayo' Zambada makes court appearance in his US drug trafficking case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, a longtime leader of Mexico’s Sinaloa cartel who is facing drug trafficking charges in a U.S. court, appeared for the first time Friday before the judge who is presiding over his case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Universal Orlando announces Epic Universe opening date
item.description: The theme park will feature more than 50 attractions, as well as entertainment, dining and shopping options across five “expansive worlds.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KTLA 5
